scenario: Trellis Bank operates Camunda 8.8 with 25000 transactions per day. An operator deletes an older process definition version from the Processes page after canceling its 12 instances that were still running. The compliance team asks what now happens to the 480000 instances of that exact version that completed earlier.
question: What happens to the finished process instances tied to that deleted process definition version?

options.text: The finished process instances tied to the deleted definition stay in Operate as a read-only historical record for the compliance team
The finished process instances tied to the deleted definition are removed from the application together with the deleted definition
The finished process instances tied to the deleted definition are migrated to the next higher version of the same process definition
The finished process instances tied to the deleted definition are exported to a separate archive cluster before the definition is removed

a.text: Incorrect. The docs explicitly list finished instances of the deleted definition as one of the resources that get deleted from the application; they are not kept as a read-only historical record.

b.text: Correct. Deleting a process definition permanently removes all of that definition's finished process instances from the application, as called out in the impact note on the Processes page docs for the delete action.

c.text: Incorrect. The delete action does not migrate finished instances to another version; migration is a separate operation and is not part of the delete flow described for the Processes page.

d.text: Incorrect. No archive-export step is part of the Processes page delete flow; finished instances of the deleted definition are removed from the application, not relocated to another cluster.

explanation: Deleting a process definition from the Processes page permanently removes the definition along with all of its finished process instances from the application, as listed in the impact note on the delete-resources docs.
